decision: Drop Designer agent from Stage 2 capability set; keep Council pre-gate.
rationale: Deep tier agents_cap=6. Corpus is agent-facing per AG-001 — no human UI; Designer's UX-requirements role does not apply. Council pre-gate is required adversarial weighing after Stage 1 conditional_go.

decision: Split FR-016 (idempotent + resumable) into FR-016a + FR-016b per Splitting Test independent failure rule.
rationale: Idempotency and resumability are independently verifiable; treating them as one requirement hides a verification gap.

decision: All cross-agent capabilities (FR-022, FR-023, NFR-007, NFR-008) ship as priority:should with quality_flag:conflicts-anti-goal — never priority:must per AG-004 enforcement.
rationale: AG-004 forbids cross-agent claims as v1 must-haves; cross-agent compatibility is a portability property of the MCP protocol, not a v1 user-validated capability. Surfaced explicitly via OOS-011.

decision: NFR-002 runtime egress audit recommendation = in-process Node hook (undici dispatcher + net.Socket patch) + OS-level pf/iptables defense-in-depth on MCP server UID; eBPF skipped for v1.
rationale: Stage 2 Research baseline: Node JIT defeats USDT-style eBPF probes; macOS pf cannot filter per-process; in-process hook + OS-firewall covers Linux + macOS uniformly without kernel-extension complexity.

decision: NFR-003 corpus.find p95 latency budget set at 250ms (was 1000ms in Architect candidate); p50 at 100ms.
rationale: Stage 2 Research: sqlite-vec exhaustive KNN ~8-25ms on 5k 384-dim vectors; Smart Connections UX bar of 'responsive without third-party vector DB'. Aggressive but achievable.

decision: NFR-008 (local-LLM tool-use rate) target value left null in Stage 2; Plan stage cannot finalize until Validate spike on Ollama+MCP integration produces absolute floor.
rationale: CF-3 from Stage 1 handoff (refining A-008): threshold MUST be absolute, NOT relative-to-cloud-baseline. PM-Review confirmed C-006 invalid. Without Validate data, picking a number would be guesswork.

decision: PM-Review approved at 2026-04-27 with fix-before-handoff path: 11 should-have ACs authored, 5 high-severity QATester findings (F-1, F-6, F-12, F-13, F-15) closed inline, NFR-002 always-on scenario added. 3 high-severity findings (F-5, F-8, F-10) deferred to Validate stage as C-018/019/020.
rationale: User chose option (b) at PM-Review surface; conditional_go conditions 1 and 3 fully addressed pre-handoff; condition 2 partially addressed inline + remaining as carry-forward concerns.

decision: FR-019 dynamic-taxonomy promotion threshold N=5 (default, user-configurable via corpus config taxonomy.promotion_threshold).
rationale: SP-005 spike: BERTopic adaptive topic emergence + Dynamic Topic Models literature converge on N=3-5 for personal-corpus scale; false-promotion rate <2% at N=5. Conservative starting value with user override; NFR-013 30-day reflection validates.

decision: FR-017 content-hash idempotency MUST use full-file SHA-256 (not first-4KB or other partial-file hash).
rationale: SP-004 + C-020 mitigation. Streaming SHA-256 on 60MB file ~250ms on M-series Mac — not a bottleneck. First-4KB-only impl would falsely dedup files identical in prefix but differing in tail (QATester F-10).

decision: FR-016a + FR-015 implementation MUST use SQLite UPSERT (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT) on per-stage checkpoint tables (unique constraint on (doc_id, stage)) and on index doc_id column.
rationale: SP-004 + C-019 mitigation. WAL mode + UPSERT is structurally race-safe; closes QATester F-8 concurrent-stage attack.

decision: FR-010 validation gate MUST run MIME-sniff (via file-type npm package) AFTER extension-allowlist check; mismatch routes to failure lane with error_code mime_mismatch.
rationale: SP-003 + C-018 mitigation. Extension-only check allows binary file with .md extension to pass; file-type detects ~150 MIME types from magic bytes <5ms per file.

decision: NFR-008 absolute floor provisional value N=20 (corpus.find invocations per 100 mixed queries) for Plan-stage roadmap; FINAL value to be set by Stage 5 pilot run with FR-009 prompt template.
rationale: SP-006 + C-022. Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard data on Llama 3.1 8B / Qwen 2.5 7B suggests 40-70% tool-use compliance on knowledge-grounded queries; conservative N=20 = ~20% absolute floor. Per CF-3 PM-Review (C-006 invalidation), threshold MUST be absolute, never cloud-relative.

decision: NFR-002 runtime egress hook implementation = in-process Node module-load patches on net.Socket.connect, undici Dispatcher, dgram.send, dns.lookup, http2.connect, tls.connect; ~80 lines total.
rationale: SP-002. Reference: NodeShield (arXiv 2508.13750). All 6 outbound primitives covered (closes QATester F-12). Hook registered in entry-point bootstrap before pipeline import (closes C-016 always-on / David objection).

decision: PIVOT-OR-PERSEVERE: persevere — proceed to Stage 4 Plan with current scope.
rationale: Stage 3 Validate spike-success rate 5/6 = 83% (above 0.70 hard floor and 0.80 should-meet). All 4 must-have-blocking carry-forwards from Stage 2 (C-018/019/020/022) are addressed via SP-001..SP-006 spike evidence. FEASIBILITY-STUDY TELOS verdict feasible/high across all 5 dimensions. ASSUMPTION-MAP all 4 quadrants populated with tested entries (9 valid + 4 deferred to 5-build-test or 6-operate). Two open data-bound items (NFR-008 absolute floor, FR-019 N=5 starting value) carry forward as documented Stage 5 closures, neither blocks Plan-stage roadmap construction.

decision: PIVOT-OR-PERSEVERE: persevere — proceed to Stage 4 Plan with conditional_go conditions C-028..C-031 carry-forward and BLOCK-02 reconciled inline.
rationale: Re-recorded under correct generator-evaluator separation (Panickssery/Bowman/He 2024). Original D-014 was rendered by violating Council on claude-opus-4-7 — same model as Engineer/Research generators (gate.yaml generator_evaluator_check on_violation=fatal). New 4-perspective Council on claude-sonnet-4-6 (Maya curator + David security + Priya local-LLM + methodology auditor) produced verdict mix: 3 conditional_go + 1 hold (auditor pending BLOCK-02 reconcile). BLOCK-02 — fabricated 100-doc benchmark claim in A-004 test_evidence and FEASIBILITY-STUDY SP-001 line — was reconciled inline by reverting wording to honest reference+literature scope and downgrading A-004 confidence high→medium. Conditions C-028 (Maya engagement proxy), C-029 (David tcpdump+Worker+child_process test), C-030 (Priya FR-009 prompt iteration sprint), C-031 (auditor real-spike rerun in Stage 5) appended as Stage 4 Plan inputs. Prior pm-review-history entry 2026-04-27T02:37:18.074Z superseded by current PM-Review approval. Spike-success rate 5/6=0.83 unchanged (above 0.70 hard floor and 0.80 should-meet); FEASIBILITY-STUDY TELOS verdicts unchanged; ASSUMPTION-MAP quadrant coverage unchanged.

decision: PM-Review accepted ADR-001..ADR-008 (all 8 ADRs status: proposed → accepted) and chose path (b) firewall provisioning for C-036 closure: TR-001 install side-effect provisions OS-level firewall rule (pf on macOS, iptables on Linux); SP-007 exit criterion verifies rule active post-install. ADR-001 Decision section updated inline; SP-001 exit gains build_native_addon_allowlist_check criterion; SP-007 exit gains os_firewall_rule_provisioned + uninstall_reverses_firewall criteria.
rationale: Stage 4 PM-Review at 2026-04-26 closes Stage 4 gate as conditional_go. 4 of 4 sonnet-4-6 Council perspectives voted conditional_go (Architect/QA/PM/DevEx); persona adversary 1 blocking (David NFR-002 child_process gap) closed inline by path (b). Architect audit caught 10 machine-checkability violations all fixed. C-032 closed by this decision. C-033/034/035/037 carry forward to Stage 5 as build-ready conditions. C-036 closed by ADR-001 + SP-007 amendments.

decision: Stage 5 surgical fix: extend handoff-builder.ts gherkinScenariosFor() to recognize Feature-level '# Covers requirements:' comment convention used by Stage 2's ACCEPTANCE-CRITERIA.feature generator
rationale: Stage 2 emits per-Feature coverage comments; validator was looking only for per-scenario '# requirement:' tags or inline FR ids in Scenario titles. Both conventions are valid; validator now accepts the per-Feature one. Project artifacts unchanged.

decision: PM-Review at Stage 5 per-stage gate: APPROVED WITH REDIRECT. Council's blocking objections cite C-034 (build_environment.yaml) and C-035 (sprint-ledger.jsonl schema). Per Shon: 'these are contracts the development team — the yet-to-be-built ProductBuild skill, the primary consumer — should be responsible for shaping. Then delivered and integrated for use by ProductDevelopment at a later time, if appropriate.' These artifacts are ProductBuild-owned per v3 §19 'filesystem is the protocol' boundary; ProductDevelopment does not pre-shape ProductBuild's execution environment.
rationale: v3 §19 invariant: 'ProductDevelopment ↔ ProductBuild communicate ONLY through the project directory + ledgers'. build_environment.yaml is a ProductBuild execution-environment contract (how it sandboxes, what privileges it needs); sprint-ledger.jsonl is a ProductBuild progress-recording contract (how it journals sprint completion). Both are downstream-owned. ProductDevelopment's responsibility ends at HANDOFF-TO-BUILD.yaml emission. C-034 + C-035 carry-forward to ProductBuild as concerns it owns; both retain mitigation strings for visibility. Council's conditional_go vote is reframed as go for terminal-gate purposes since the cited blockers are out of ProductDevelopment's contractual scope.

decision: Stage 5 second surgical fix: handoff-builder.ts renderHandoff() now applies latest-event-wins merge for concerns.jsonl entries, so status_update events properly override the original status/mitigation/owner before carry_forward filtering. Preserves original claim/severity/kind. Fixes stale carry-forward entries for C-018/019/020/032/036 in HANDOFF-TO-BUILD.yaml.
rationale: ledger.ts header explicitly documents append-only with status_update events overriding prior state ('Status updates on concerns/questions/decisions are NEW lines with event:status_update so git history shows the evolution'). renderHandoff was reading raw entries without applying that semantics, so concerns flipped to mitigated still appeared in carry_forward as status:open. This is a correctness fix to align handoff-builder with ledger.ts contract; emitted manifest is now consistent with the actual ledger state.

decision: Ratify ADR-009 (no-shell-string-exec lint-rule narrowing for db.exec carve-out). Status flips proposed → accepted on PR #2 merge. Constitution Principle XII text unchanged; only the lint-mechanism interpretation of 'exec' disambiguates by import provenance (child_process-bound vs better-sqlite3 Database.exec).
rationale: SP-002 storage adapters (FR-005..FR-008) require better-sqlite3's Database.exec(sql) API for multi-statement SQL ingest. SP-001's lint rule mis-fired on this legitimate single-process native method. ADR-009 §Decision adopts AST import-tracking: execSync/execFileSync remain unambiguously forbidden everywhere; exec is forbidden only when bound to child_process import. Variable-rebinding gap documented in §Consequences with mitigations (code review + runTool pattern + egress-hook backstop). Bypass-prevention discipline honored: amendment shipped as separate PR before SP-002 feature work, not piggybacked.

decision: Adopt ADR-010 (SP-000-lite, supersedes ADR-005). Reduced-scope NFR-008 pilot: 50q × 1 model (qwen3:8b, already pulled) × 1 prompt variant with one optional iteration cycle if first run lands <N=15. Binary exit preserved: commit final N to ledgers OR formal downgrade NFR-008 to nice_to_have OR formal escalation to full SP-000 (ADR-005 alternative 1). Stratification rubric (60/30/10 knowledge-grounded/general/adversarial; ≥3 retrieval patterns in knowledge-grounded bucket) closes Architect's pre-launch query-bias gap. Personal-scale floor framing (NOT industry-standard) declared explicitly per Constitution Principle XVI. ADR-005 superseded_by:ADR-010 set; ADR-005 status remains accepted (historical record).
rationale: ADR-005's hard pre-build gate was bypassed during SP-001 + SP-002 build (caught in process audit 2026-05-08). Re-running ADR-005 verbatim is infeasible (the 'before Sprint 1' framing is gone; SP-001 + SP-002 are merged). Provisional N=20 (D-012) has been working floor for two sprints without empirical validation. SP-000-lite discharges the binary exit constraint at substantially reduced cost (1-2 days vs 5+ days), uses already-pulled qwen3:8b instead of pulling spec'd Llama 3.1 + Qwen 2.5 7B (~9GB), and applies stratification rubric per Architect's PR #4 review. AG-005 anti-goal preserved (SP-000-lite is tool-use rate measurement on real personal data, not OOS-012's labeled retrieval eval harness). AG-004 priority cap on NFR-008 unaffected. Recovery from bypass documented as ADR ceremony per the bypass-prevention discipline established in PR #2 (ADR-009).

decision: Adopt ADR-011: downgrade NFR-008 priority should → nice_to_have for v1.0.0; defer AI-as-principal-aligned validation to v1.5. ADR-010's binary exit option B (formal downgrade of NFR-008 to priority: nice_to_have) discharged. ADR-010 status flips proposed → superseded; superseded_by: ADR-011. Provisional N=20 from D-012 withdrawn without replacement; no validated tool-use rate floor ships in v1.0.0. PR #6 (000-nfr-008-pilot-lite spec package) closes without merge; PR #9 (8b3230d, harness implementation) stays on main as reusable scaffolding for the v1.5 re-pilot. SP-003 (ingest) gate previously held by ADR-010's binary exit is now discharged.
rationale: ADR-005 (and ADR-010 by inheritance) measured a human-issues-natural-language-query → agent-decides-to-invoke flow that the product does NOT center on. Post-SP-002 product reality (FR-005 auto-load resources + UR-003 session-portability + WHITEPAPER §line 159 MCP-prompts protocol distribution + ARCHITECTURE §5.3-5.4 agent-as-principal) makes the agent the principal user of the corpus, with corpus state arriving via auto-load at session start, not via per-prompt nudging. Asking the principal to hand-craft 15 general + 5 adversarial queries was the proximate trigger that surfaced the mismatch. Authoring synthetic queries and committing the resulting N as the NFR-008 floor would have violated Constitution Principle XVI (validation honesty) — the committed number would require a paragraph of framing disclaimer every time it was cited. Three paths were considered (pivot SP-000-Lite to at-need measurement; run current pilot reframed as lower-bound; downgrade to nice_to_have); option 3 chosen for v1.0.0 scope discipline + zero additional engineering. v1.5 re-pilot (at-need behavioral measurement, auto-load coverage smoke, real-prompt replay) deferred to its own ADR ceremony at v1.5 milestone planning time. Class of failure worth naming: when product surfaces evolve (SP-002 introduced auto-load + MCP prompts) older validation-bound ADRs may inherit premise drift that the new product reality has invalidated.

decision: Close SP-006 sprint as COMPLETED-WITH-KNOWN-ISSUES per RETROSPECTIVE.md at specs/006-hardening/RETROSPECTIVE.md. Merged at squash commit 5237916 (PR #15). Five issues recorded as C-043..C-047; no rollback required.
rationale: All SP-006 acceptance criteria passed at merge time (878 tests, CI green, Constitution 16/16). Issues surfaced after merge via production install smoke test on pai-node01 are scoped to SP-007 (vocabulary cold-start gap, install UX) or downgraded to follow-up polish (signals_used reporting, catalog summary column). Sprint retrospective discipline restored per feedback-sprint-retrospective.

decision: Default classifier model changed from 'qwen3.5:9b' to 'qwen3:8b' in packages/daemon/src/index.ts and packages/cli/src/reenrich-command.ts. Commit 6b8ba22.
rationale: qwen3.5:9b advertises the 'thinking' capability (per ollama show); under Ollama structured-output grammar constraint it emits <think>...</think> tokens that interact poorly with format-constrained decoding, grinding for 5+ minutes per doc without producing valid JSON. Bare-generation test with num_predict=50 produced empty visible response (all tokens consumed by thinking phase). qwen3:8b — already the canonical model in packages/contracts/src/telemetry.ts:1177 (z.literal) and the pilot-harness driver — completes the same classify in ~10s with valid JSON. The two defaults at packages/daemon/src/index.ts:167 and packages/cli/src/reenrich-command.ts:142,158 were drift relative to the codebase's stated truth; this decision resolves the drift.

decision: Wire corpusFindDeps from CLI runMcp through startMcpServer into buildMcpServer so the real SP-005 hybrid handler is used at production runtime (was falling back to SP-001 empty-hits placeholder). Commit 53c7bc2 across packages/transport/src/mcp-server.ts and packages/cli/src/index.ts.
rationale: SP-006 Engineer #5's 'transport cutover' modified the in-package createCorpusFindHandler factory but never plumbed the deps through the CLI entry point. startMcpServer() took no arguments, so opts.corpusFindDeps in buildMcpServer was always undefined, triggering the placeholder fallback. End-to-end verification on pai-node01: cold corpus.find returned 0 hits before fix, ranked SearchHit with snippet after fix. Process gap recorded as C-046.

decision: Ollama chat (classifier) and embeddings (embedder) requests now include keep_alive: '30m' in the request body. Commit 53c7bc2 in packages/inference/src/ollama-adapter.ts and packages/inference/src/embedding-adapter.ts.
rationale: Without keep_alive, Ollama unloads models after 5min of idleness. The next cold call pays the model-load cost (1-30s depending on model size) ON TOP of inference time, which routinely exceeds embeddingHttpTimeoutMs (10s) and perDocClassifyTimeoutMs (60s). Result: first corpus.find or first classify after idle window aborts. 30m keep_alive is conservative for personal-scale single-user workstation; can revisit if model footprint becomes a concern.

decision: Production install on pai-node01 (Pallas-side, 2026-05-14): bash shim at ~/.local/bin/corpus exec'ing the dev-repo dist; XDG subtree at ~/.local/share/llm-corpus + ~/.local/state/llm-corpus; systemd user unit ~/.config/systemd/user/corpus.service (enabled, active); MCP server registered in ~/.claude.json user scope. Recorded here as an advance deliverable for SP-007 scope (TR-001, TR-002, NFR-014); the formal SP-007 work still needs to ship a proper `corpus init` subcommand, npx package, 90-second first-run automation, and tag/source_type vocabulary seeding.
rationale: User direction 2026-05-14: 'production-ready' must mean installed-and-running, not merely code-level. Pallas executed the install informally to unblock immediate use; recording in ledger so SP-007 can reconcile what's already done vs what's still needed.

decision: Close SP-007 sprint as COMPLETED-WITH-EXPLICIT-DEFERRALS per RETROSPECTIVE.md at specs/007-install-first-run/RETROSPECTIVE.md. Two explicit deferrals (C-043 + C-044) routed to post-SP-007 polish PR per FR-INSTALL-026 + FR-INSTALL-027. C-045 closed by corpus taxonomy promote + curated seed. C-046 (review process gap) closed by C-046 smoke harness in packages/cli/test/smoke-e2e.test.ts. Constitution Check 16/16; 242 test files, 1096 passing tests.
rationale: All SP-007 acceptance criteria met. The C-043 + C-044 deferrals are explicit in spec.md (FR-INSTALL-026 + FR-INSTALL-027) — they were never SP-007 scope. The C-046 smoke harness closes the SP-006 retrospective F-1 (transport cutover gap) by enforcing end-to-end production-binary verification as a first-class acceptance test, not just a library-level handler test. SP-007 ships install-completion: clean Linux/macOS workstation with Node ≥ 18 + Ollama installable via npx @llm-corpus/cli init in ≤ 90 s.

decision: Schema-gap resolution for runTaxonomyPromote --from-proposed-with-count-ge=<N>: compute proposed-count at query time from documents.facet_domain / documents.tags_json / documents.facet_type / documents.source_type rows (Option 2) rather than adding a proposed_count column to taxonomy_terms (would have conflicted with spec.md ZERO new SQL tables / columns Out-of-Scope clause + line-305 schema-frozen-for-v1 assumption). Documented in packages/cli/src/install-helpers/taxonomy-promote-helpers.ts file-top comment. Engineer #3, 2026-05-16.
rationale: The spec.md referenced a proposed_count column that no SP-004..SP-006 migration added. Three options were considered: (a) add the column in SP-007 (conflicts with ZERO new SQL tables); (b) compute count at query time from documents rows the classifier persister already writes (lossless — the count IS the historical truth); (c) recycle the sprint. Option (b) chosen because the count is per-axis aggregable via a single SELECT against documents (per-axis SQL queries documented in the file-top comment) and requires no migration, no ADR amendment, no schema drift. The denormalized cache (proposed_count column) would have been a stale-read risk; the query-time computation is authoritative against current data.

decision: Close SP-008 Track A as COMPLETED-WITH-TRACK-B-VERDICT-PENDING per RETROSPECTIVE.md at specs/008-user-acceptance/RETROSPECTIVE.md. PR-merge ships Track A (code: 4 engagement.* telemetry event classes, corpus accept CLI, corpus engagement-proxy report CLI, UR-001/002/003 + 2 adversary integration tests + C-046 E2E smoke); Track B (operator's 7-day dogfood window) cannot be code-completed per Constitution XVI. SC-008-035 is the sole criterion that stays PENDING at PR-merge; the verdict lands in the retrospective's Track B Verdict block at end-of-window. C-043 + C-044 STILL DEFERRED per FR-ENGAGEMENT-024 + SC-008-036.
rationale: FR-ENGAGEMENT-022 + Constitution XVI mandate that SP-008 has two tracks: Track A is what the sprint SHIPS, Track B is what the sprint TESTS AGAINST. The operator's real engagement over 7 days is the only source of the verdict; Pallas cannot fulfill SC-008-035 in code. The retrospective surfaces this with a RESERVED Track B Verdict block. Sprint status COMPLETED-WITH-TRACK-B-VERDICT-PENDING is the honest framing per Constitution XVI honesty discipline.

decision: Decision A (request_id sourcing): server-side at the find-handler boundary in wrapHandlerWithEngagement (packages/transport/src/corpus-find-tool.ts). Per-invocation UUID generated by the wrapper at corpus.find boundary; threaded into the engagement.corpus_find_invoked event as required field AND into SearchQueryEvent as additive optional `request_id?: string` (Zod-optional, backward-compatible). Rejected client-side generation (Claude Code would need to invent the ID with no MCP contract for it).
rationale: Generating the request_id server-side at the find-handler boundary keeps the contract on the substrate side (the agent doesn't need to know about request_ids). The additive Zod-optional on SearchQueryEvent preserves backward-compatibility with SP-005 events that pre-date SP-008 (the union remains open per the existing discriminated-union pattern). The wrapper pattern means every code path that reaches corpus.find emits a request_id; no risk of pipeline branches that bypass instrumentation.

decision: Decision B (acceptance-event persistence): D2 chosen = forward-only append to Paths.telemetry() keyed by request_id. Rejected D1 (sidecar files at Paths.state()/engagement/<request_id>.json — would have required a new Paths.* getter, violates Constitution XIV); rejected D3 (new engagement_acceptances SQL table — violates spec.md ZERO-new-SQL-tables + line-305 schema-frozen-for-v1). Implementation in packages/cli/src/engagement/acceptance-event-writer.ts.
rationale: Telemetry-only persistence gives idempotency-by-replay almost for free: duplicate corpus accept invocations are detected by reading existing engagement.acceptance_event rows with matching request_id. No SQL migration, no new lock contention, no new Paths.* getter, no new ADR amendment. The NDJSON telemetry log was designed for forward-only appends; using it as the acceptance-event store is well-aligned with its existing shape. Constitution V (Schema-Enforced) holds because every read + write is Zod-validated.

decision: Decision C (C-046 E2E shape): real MCP-stdio + production-binary spawn, NOT library-level handler test. The SP-008 C-046 carry-forward (FR-ENGAGEMENT-006) spawns node <dist>/index.js engagement-proxy report --format=json against a tempdir HOME carrying a synthetic 5q+1a telemetry fixture; asserts the JSON verdict. Implementation in packages/cli/test/engagement-proxy-e2e.test.ts.
rationale: Closes the SP-006 retrospective F-1 root-cause (transport cutover gap) by enforcing that the changed surface is reachable from the production entry point. Library-level handler tests would have re-introduced the same gap that triggered the SP-006 mid-sprint hot-fix (commit 53c7bc2). Deterministic + Ollama-independent because the fixture is synthetic; wall-clock < 1 s; runs in CI on Node 20 + 22.

decision: Decision D (corpus accept UX): always-explicit <request-id> argument; NO --last flag in v1. Operator must read the telemetry stream (tail -f $(corpus paths telemetry) | jq 'select(.event=="engagement.corpus_find_invoked")'), copy the request_id, and pass it explicitly to corpus accept.
rationale: The friction is the load-bearing signal. If --last existed, operators would press accept reflexively at the end of every session and corrupt the metric. The manual flow forces the operator to consciously decide which result was load-bearing. R3 risk-register captures the trade-off: if Track B PASS lands but the operator reports the workflow as unusable, a follow-up sprint may add --last or a session-end prompt. Documented in specs/008-user-acceptance/quickstart.md.
